story.iWant: implement API endpoints and services for creating, reading, updating, and deleting projects
story.soThat: the application can manage project lifecycle operations through tRPC procedures
story.tasks: - Create projects router file with Zod schemas - Implement projects.create procedure with validation - Implement projects.list procedure - Implement projects.get procedure - Implement projects.delete procedure with cascade handling - Integrate projects router into appRouter - Add validation logic (directory, git, uniqueness) - Manual testing (no test framework yet in Epic 1)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: projects.create tRPC procedure creates new project entry
projects.list tRPC procedure lists all projects
projects.get tRPC procedure gets single project with details
projects.delete tRPC procedure removes project (with confirmation)
Project creation validates: directory path exists or can be created, directory is empty or has valid git repository, project name is unique
Project deletion: removes database entries (cascade delete for related records), does NOT delete files on disk (safety measure), warns user about active agents
All operations return proper tRPC error codes and error messages

doc.title: Database Schema - Final Design
doc.snippet: projects table schema (lines 53-76): uuid id, text name (unique), text path, project_level enum, project_type enum, field_type enum, timestamps. Index on name for lookups.

doc.title: Database Schema - Final Design
doc.snippet: project_state table (lines 79-100): Links to projects.id with onDelete: cascade. Contains workflow tracking. Cascade delete ensures cleanup on project deletion.

doc.title: Architecture Summary
doc.snippet: Backend: Hono framework, tRPC for type-safe API, PostgreSQL with Drizzle ORM, Bun runtime. Frontend: React + TypeScript + Vite.

doc.title: Tool Stack Decision
doc.snippet: AI SDK (Vercel) approved for LLM operations with Zod schemas for structured outputs. Mastra for workflow state. Use tRPC for API layer with type safety.

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: Story 1.3 acceptance criteria (lines 143-157): API endpoints for projects CRUD, validation requirements, cascade delete behavior, safety measures for deletion.

doc.title: Previous Story - Workflow Seeding
doc.snippet: Drizzle ORM patterns: db.query.*.findFirst() for lookups, .onConflictDoNothing() for upsert, foreign key validation before inserts. Use try-catch with TRPCError throwing.

artifact.reason: Projects table schema with uuid id, unique name constraint, level/type/fieldType enums. Required for CRUD operations.
Project state table with cascade delete. Demonstrates foreign key relationship and automatic cleanup on project deletion.
Example tRPC router with publicProcedure and protectedProcedure patterns. Template for projects router structure.
tRPC middleware pattern for auth. Shows how to use TRPCError for authorization failures. Pattern to follow for validation errors.
tRPC context creation with auth session. Base context available in all procedures. Use ctx.session for user identification.
API package dependencies: @trpc/server, hono, zod (catalog versions), @chiron/auth and @chiron/db workspace references.

constraints.constraint: Use tRPC procedures, not plain Hono routes - project uses tRPC for type-safe API layer
Create router in packages/api/src/routers/projects.ts following existing router pattern
Use Zod schemas for input validation - all tRPC inputs must have Zod schema
Use publicProcedure for now - auth not required for Epic 1 MVP
Throw TRPCError with appropriate codes: BAD_REQUEST, NOT_FOUND, INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
Use Drizzle ORM for database operations - no raw SQL
Project deletion must NOT delete files on disk - database only
Cascade delete handled by database schema (onDelete: cascade) - no manual cleanup
Directory validation: check if path exists, can be created, is empty or has .git directory
Project name must be unique - database has unique constraint, handle conflict errors
Manual testing only for Epic 1 - no formal test framework configured yet

interface.name: projects.create
projects.get
interface.signature: input: z.object({ id: z.string().uuid() }) output: { project: Project }

interface.name: projects.delete
interface.signature: input: z.object({ id: z.string().uuid() }) output: { success: boolean }

interface.name: Drizzle ORM Query API
interface.signature: db.query.projects.findFirst({ where: eq(projects.id, id) }) db.query.projects.findMany() db.insert(projects).values({ ... }).returning() db.delete(projects).where(eq(projects.id, id))

tests.standards: No formal test framework configured for Epic 1. Use manual API testing with tools like curl, Postman, Thunder Client, or write simple validation scripts. Focus on verifying acceptance criteria through manual execution.
tests.locations: No test directories yet. Testing infrastructure planned for Epic 2+.

ideas.idea: Manual test: Call projects.create with valid data, verify project created in database
Manual test: Call projects.create with duplicate name, verify error returned
Manual test: Call projects.create with invalid path, verify validation error
Manual test: Call projects.list, verify all projects returned
Manual test: Call projects.get with valid ID, verify project details returned
Manual test: Call projects.get with invalid ID, verify NOT_FOUND error
Manual test: Call projects.delete, verify project removed from database
Manual test: After delete, verify project directory still exists on disk
Manual test: After delete, verify project_state entry auto-removed (cascade)
Manual test: Verify all error responses have proper tRPC error codes and messages
